scenario: A Tasklist user has 50 pending tasks. They want to filter to tasks with urgency = "HIGH" to focus on urgent work first.
question: Does Tasklist support filtering by task variables?

options.text: Tasklist only supports filtering tasks by candidate group assignment, with no ability to filter by task variables or other criteria -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Tasklist provides multiple filter dimensions including assignee, candidate group, process definition, task status, due date, follow-up date, task ID, and task variable name-value pairs. The combination of these filters enables targeted task views that surface the most relevant work items for each user or team.

The Tasklist web UI includes variable-based filtering in its task filter panel, allowing users to specify variable name and value pairs to narrow the displayed tasks. This UI-level variable filtering enables business users to focus on urgent or high-priority tasks without needing API access or technical knowledge of query construction.

Tasklist has no filtering capability and always displays all pending tasks regardless of assignment, status, or content -- this description does not reflect the actual behaviour. Filtering is a core feature of Tasklist's user interface, not an optional add-on. The task list view provides a comprehensive filter panel covering assignee, candidate group, process, status, dates, task ID, and task variable values. md, users can save named filters locally for repeated use. The Tasklist V2 REST API mirrors the same filtering for programmatic access.

Yes -- Tasklist's task list view supports filtering by task variable name and value pairs, in addition to standard filters such as assignee, candidate group, state, due date, and follow-up date. To focus on urgent tasks: configure a variable filter with name urgency and value HIGH; the task list narrows to only matching tasks. md, users can also save named filter configurations locally for repeated use.

a.text: Incorrect. Per using-filters.md:33-38 Tasklist's filter dialog supports six attribute classes: Assignees and candidate groups Status Processes tasks belong to Dates (due date and follow-up date) Task ID Task variables (name + value match -- the key one for the scenario) Combining attributes narrows the visible task list. Variable filtering is critical for business-context filtering -- "show me only urgent claims," "show me only orders over $1000," etc. Filters can be saved by name per using-filters.md:44-56, but per :58-60 note: "Filters you create are saved locally to your device" -- they don't roam across devices. Tasklist V2 REST API (POST /v2/user-tasks/search) mirrors the same filtering for programmatic access. Performance: similar to Operate, ES-backed; variable filters' speed depends on indexing + selectivity.
